content.description:
Context menus are Hobson's primary interaction mode. Right-clicking an item reveals operations like changing views, adding children, duplicating, deleting, and more. Views can extend the menu with their own items, making the context menu a composable extension point.

## Triggering

| Gesture | Effect |
|---------|--------|
| Right-click on an item | Item context menu |
| Right-click on empty background | Background menu (Search, Open REPL) |
| Shift+Right-click | Bypass Hobson menu, show browser's native context menu |

The [viewport-view](item://bd74da77-a459-454a-b001-48685d4b536d) listens for `contextmenu` events on the container. It walks up the DOM from the click target to find the nearest `[data-item-id]` element. If found, it shows the item menu; otherwise, the background menu.

## Standard Item Menu

The viewport provides these built-in operations:

| Menu Item | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| **Add Child** | Submenu: "New Item..." or "Existing Item..." |
| **View As...** | Submenu listing all available views for the item's type. Current view marked with ✓. If the active view exports `getViewMenuItems()`, those appear as a nested submenu. |
| **View Settings...** | Opens a modal showing the three-level view preference hierarchy (context → item → type) with controls to set or clear each level. |
| **Copy ID** | Copies item GUID to clipboard |
| **Duplicate** | Creates a copy of the item |
| **Make Root** | Promotes item to viewport root, preserving its view config |
| **Export as JSON** | Downloads item as a `.json` file |
| **Delete** | Removes item (detaches from parent and deletes from storage) |
| **Debug** | Submenu: debug views (e.g., Raw JSON), Enable Debug Mode toggle, Toggle Inspector (when debug mode active) |

## Background Menu

Right-clicking empty space shows a minimal menu:

- **Search Items (Cmd+K)** — opens the [item palette](item://f1111111-0005-0000-0000-000000000000)
- **Open REPL (Esc)** — toggles the REPL panel

## View Menu Extensions

Views can contribute menu items by exporting a `getViewMenuItems()` function. These items appear as a submenu under **View As... → [Current View]**.

```javascript
// In a view's code item:
export async function getViewMenuItems(item, api) {
  return [
    {
      label: 'My Option',
      checked: false,
      onClick: async () => { /* do something */ }
    },
    { separator: true },
    {
      label: 'Another Option',
      onClick: async () => { /* ... */ }
    }
  ];
}
```

Each menu item can have:

| Property | Type | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| `label` | String | Display text |
| `onClick` | Function | Action when clicked |
| `checked` | Boolean | Shows ✓ or highlight if true |
| `disabled` | Boolean | Grays out the item |
| `separator` | Boolean | Renders a divider line instead of a clickable item |

### Example: Spatial Canvas

The [spatial-canvas-view](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) uses `getViewMenuItems()` to let users choose a background view. It also provides its own **window menu** (via the ≡ button on each window's title bar) with Pin, Dock, Unpin, and Undock operations — these are internal to the view, not part of the viewport's menu system.

## Submenus

Menu items can have children, rendered as hover-activated submenus. Submenus flip to the left side when the menu is near the right edge of the viewport (detected via `submenu-left` CSS class).

## Styling

Context menus are styled by the [context-menu-css](item://d4e149ab-35fa-44a4-a858-779015bdee70) item. Key properties:
- Fixed positioning at click coordinates (adjusted to stay within viewport)
- z-index `2147483647` (above everything, including maximized windows)
- Standard `.context-menu`, `.context-menu-item`, `.context-menu-separator`, `.context-menu-submenu` classes

## Selection

The context menu works with the [selection system](item://f1111111-0001-0000-0000-000000000000). When a context menu targets an item, that item becomes selected (highlighted with `.item-selected` class). Selection tracks both the item ID and its parent ID for context-aware operations.

## Design Rationale

### Why Right-Click as Primary Mode?

Hobson avoids persistent toolbars and ribbon UIs. Right-click provides contextual operations without consuming screen space. The menu adapts to the item — different types and views surface different options.

### Why Composable?

Since views are userland items, the viewport can't know in advance what operations a view might offer. The `getViewMenuItems()` protocol lets any view contribute to the menu without modifying the viewport. This follows the same principle as declarative watches — new behavior without kernel changes.

### Why Shift+Right-Click Escape Hatch?

Browser developer tools and extensions depend on the native context menu. The Shift modifier provides a clean escape hatch.

## See Also

- [viewport-view](item://bd74da77-a459-454a-b001-48685d4b536d) — Menu implementation
- [context-menu-css](item://d4e149ab-35fa-44a4-a858-779015bdee70) — Menu styling
- [Viewport](item://c0c0c0c0-0033-0000-0000-000000000000) — The top-level container that hosts the menu
- [Rendering & Views](item://c0c0c0c0-0022-0000-0000-000000000000) — View resolution and the "View As" menu
- [Spatial Windowing](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) — Window-level menus in the spatial canvas
